judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After retrieving all articles from the MongoDB collection, the agent’s execute_python step used a naive keyword-based sports classifier (sports_keywords including generic terms like "goal") to filter articles, misclassifying non-sports content and thus selecting "Making RSS Scale" instead of the true longest-description sports article.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the contributing execute_python step, the agent classified “sports” articles via naive substring keyword matching (e.g., checking if any of ['football','run','match',…] appear in the title/description) and then chose the longest description, which misclassified a non-sports article and led to returning "Outsourcing issue flares up at chip conference" instead of the true sports article "The Rundown".
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After retrieving articles, the agent implemented a keyword-based sports classifier using naive substring checks ("if kw in text") that falsely labeled "Wall St. Bears Claw Back Into the Black (Reuters)" as Sports because "win" matched inside "dwindling," and then selected it as the longest, yielding the wrong title instead of the true sports article "The Rundown."
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@
